titleStmt.title: Letter to Sir John Robert O'Connell, 1 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter was addressed to Sir John Robert O'Connell (1868-1943), a solicitor who was applying for the position of chief crown solicitor. In the letter O'Connell is thanked for enclosing references from Cardinal Michael Logue (1840-1924), Archbishop of Armagh, and John Harty(1867-1946) , Archbishop of Cashel, in reference to his application for the chief crown solicitor position.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/7153/22

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
1st April, 1916. Dear Sir John, Thanks for your further note of this
morning enclosing letters from His Eminence the Cardinal
Archbishop of Armagh and His Grace the Archbishop
of Cashel in reference to your application for the
Office of Chief Crown Solicitor for Ireland. Yours sincerely, Sir John Robert O'Connell, 34, Kildare Street,
Dublin.
